current_config_replay.evidence: config/current_alpha_watchlist.json has no item whose symbol is AKE
current_config_replay.result: current generic monitors do not build an AKE event

existing_capabilities.capability: builds events only from active P0/P1/P2 watchlist items with a valid BSC contract and pool
can classify one receipt as a sell when one configured quote token is net-positive to the seller in the same receipt
uses a recent 1800-block default window, receipt candidate selection, and a 20-second receipt-sampling deadline
can confirm direct or bounded next-hop quote recovery for selected opening buyers using the configured quote token
opening analysis defaults to 25 selected transactions and four traced meaningful buyers

observed_gaps.detail: AKE is absent from the current watchlist, so the generic runtime path cannot replay this case without an explicit review event
the current cluster logic covers CEX withdrawal fan-out; it does not persist arbitrary multi-sender fan-in to a common EOA and connect that address to later quote recovery
126 sell receipts plus other token transactions exceed the intraday 120-receipt candidate cap, and the 20-second deadline may reduce sampled receipts further
runtime sell classification follows one configured quote token; this case recovered USDT plus USDC and WBNB dust across the batch
opening buyer tracing defaults to the latest three outgoing transactions per traced buyer and cannot reconstruct 126 sells as one complete batch

expected_runtime_behavior_if_configured_.some_large_sell_activity: likely detectable when selected before deadline
expected_runtime_behavior_if_configured_.usdc_and_wbnb_dust_aggregation: not represented by the single configured quote field

change_decision.reason: the positive case proves a real coverage gap, while a safe generic fan-in rule still lacks negative fixtures for ordinary retail pooling, router or custody routing, non-sale transfers, and partial receipt coverage
recommended_fixture_set: positive: multi-sender fan-in followed by complete same-receipt token-out and quote-in batch
negative: fan-in followed only by custody or internal routing with no quote recovery
negative: ordinary unrelated deposits into one address
negative: router call or token balance decline without quote received by the seller
coverage: receipt cap or RPC failure leaves the batch incomplete and report-only

strategy_effect.reason: existing celue rules already require receipt or next-hop quote recovery before confirmed sell and keep common control unresolved
strategy_effect.case_value: positive design fixture for a future coverage-aware fan-in-to-confirmed-sell path
